page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the N1 machine family. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, and the instance is equipped with 60.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not include bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also register poor performance. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by average ratings in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, ray tracing, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth across both small and large blocks consistently performs in the average tier.

Given its balanced resource profile and average multi-threaded performance, the n1-standard-16 is suited for general-purpose workloads that can leverage parallel processing. These include static web serving, database operations, and software compilation, which all achieved average benchmark ratings. It can also handle light LLM inference tasks for smaller models, though performance degrades on larger models. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and standard memory-to-core ratio offer a predictable, mid-tier resource density for steady-state workloads that do not require high single-threaded performance or local NVMe storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs and 60.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It utilizes 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing a dedicated CPU allocation. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals a significant contrast between single-core and multi-core performance: single-threaded operations, such as gzip compression and Geekbench single-core tests, rank in the lower percentiles, whereas multi-threaded operations, software compilation, and database operations achieve average performance tiers. Memory bandwidth remains stable and average across both read and write tests. This instance is best suited for parallelizable, general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, multi-threaded software compilation, and moderate database operations where high single-core speed is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 60 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It delivers average multi-core performance for standard parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-16 is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 60 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine offering sixteen virtual cores and sixty gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 60.0 GB of RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 2.3 GHz processor
Average multi-core performance suitable for parallel processing tasks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Well-suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
Lower single-core performance limits efficiency for single-threaded applications
